reasoning: In 2021, the World Bank’s Global Findex database reported that account ownership in Nigeria was approximately 45%. Reaching 95% by 2024 would require a 50 percentage point increase in three years, an expansion rate unprecedented for any developing nation, let alone one facing significant infrastructure, poverty, and rural access barriers. The 2023 EFInA Access to Financial Services survey—a leading benchmark for Nigeria's financial inclusion—reported formal financial inclusion at roughly 64%, with overall inclusion (including informal services) at 74%. This confirms that the ambitious 95% target set by the Central Bank of Nigeria in 2019 was missed by a wide margin. Given this definitive data published well into the timeline, it is virtually impossible that 95% of adult Nigerians had an account by the onset of 2025.
